coredata.dc:title: Facile synthesis of graphene quantum dots from corn powder and their application as down conversion effect in quantum dot-dye-sensitized solar cell
coredata.dc:creator: Teymourinia, Hakimeh
Salavati-Niasari, Masoud
Amiri, Omid
Farangi, Mostafa
coredata.dc:description: Graphene quantum dots are interesting materials that are currently attracting world-wide scientific and technological interest because of their unique optical properties. In this research, graphene quantum dots were prepared from corn powder and were applied in DSSCs as down conversion materials. These prepared graphene quantum dots convert UV light to 450 and 520nm light which favorable for DSSCs. Modified solar cell based on graphene quantum dots shows 21% enhancement in JSC compare to the reference cell. EIS, IMPS and IMVS have used to approve that this enhancement is because of down conversion effect of graphene quantum dots. The results of our study offer a novel down conversion material which have many advantages compare to the previous reports. These results could be used in other photovoltaic systems.
coredata.dcterms:subject: EIS
IMVS
Dye synthesized solar cell
Down conversion
Photovoltaic

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Preparation of working electrodes and cell assembling

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: X-ray diffraction pattern for as-synthesized graphene quantum dots by using corn powder.
The scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images of GQDs1 GQDs2.
a) PL, UV-vis and PLE results, b) the ultraviolet-visible (UV-vis) absorption spectrum and c) Pl of GQDs.
J–V curve of different devices including references cell, GQDs1, and GQDs2.
Electrochemical impedance spectra for devices containing: references cell, based on GQDs1 and GQDs2.
Possible mechanism for enhance efficiency in present of graphene quantum dots.
Solar cell performance parameters of fabricated devices under AM 1.5.
Electrochemical parameters obtained by fitting the impedance spectra of DSSCs.
IMPS and IMVS results (transport, recombination times, and charge-collection efficiency) for reference cell, GQD1 and GQD2.

head.ce:title: Facile synthesis of graphene quantum dots from corn powder and their application as down conversion effect in quantum dot-dye-sensitized solar cell

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Institute of Nano Science and Nano Technology, University of Kashan, Kashan, P. O. Box. 87317-51167, I. R., Iran

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Institute of Nano Science and Nano Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, USA

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Young and Elite Researchers Club, Arak Branch, Islamic Azad University, Arak, Iran

ce:affiliation.ce:label: d

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Graphene quantum dots are interesting materials that are currently attracting world-wide scientific and technological interest because of their unique optical properties. In this research, graphene quantum dots were prepared from corn powder and were applied in DSSCs as down conversion materials. These prepared graphene quantum dots convert UV light to 450 and 520nm light which favorable for DSSCs. Modified solar cell based on graphene quantum dots shows 21% enhancement in JSC compare to the reference cell. EIS, IMPS and IMVS have used to approve that this enhancement is because of down conversion effect of graphene quantum dots. The results of our study offer a novel down conversion material which have many advantages compare to the previous reports. These results could be used in other photovoltaic systems.

ce:section.ce:para: Dye-sensitized solar cells (DSSCs) have attracted attention as a renewable and green source of energy due to low cost, high performance, easy fabrication procedures, and so on [1–3]. Typically, Dye-sensitized solar cells consist of a wide band gap semiconductor (TiO2) as photo-anode, sensitizer, an iodine-based electrolyte and Pt as cathode electrode [4]. One problem that limits the power conversion efficiency (PCE) of DSSCS is low light absorption [5,6]. Till now, many efforts have done to extend light absorption and PCE. Recently, Omid Amiri et al. report significant improvement in PCE of dye-sensitized solar cells by using gold and silver nanoparticles [7–9]. They used these noble metals and CdS QDs to enhance light harvesting. Using up and down conversion materials is another intelligently choice to improve light harvesting and improve PCE. For example Woo Kim and coworkers in 2016 used NaYF4:Yb3+,Er3+ to enhance light harvesting in DSSCs. Their modified cell shows 19.5% improvement compare to reference cells [10]. Another instance is an articles published by Zahra Hosseinia et al. in 2017 which CaAlSiN3:Eu2+ was used as down conversion material to improve light harvesting in DSSCs [11]. They report 64% enhancement in the short circuit current density when CaAlSiN3:Eu2+ was used in solar cells. Varity of materials such as NaYF4:Er3+, β-NaYF4:Yb3+ (18%)/Er3+, YVO4:Bi3+, La3:Ce3+/Tb3+, LaVO4:Dy3+ and so have been used as up/down conversion materials [12–16].

Down conversion, is a luminescence process where absorb two or more high energy UV photons and then convert them to a low energy visible photon while Up conversion absorb two or more low energy infrared photons and then convert them to a high energy visible photon. These compounds have complicated synthetic methods and toxic elements [17]. In this work, we introduce a new compound as down shifted nanoparticles in DSSCs for the first time. Herein, we use graphene quantum dots (GQDs) as down conversion materials which convert UV light to the 450nm light that is appropriate for DSSCs. GQDs are small non toxicity and environmental friendship carbon nanoparticles with the size less than 10nm. In this research, GQDs was prepared by green method and precursor (corn powder as precursor).

In addition, we use a new design in using down conversion material in DSSCS. Previous reports used down conversion materials (DC) before photo anode or on after photo anode (inside the cell). In the case of using DC before photoanode, it may scatter light before it goes to the cell and reduce the amount of accessible photon into the cells. In the case of using DC after photoanode, it is clear that emitted light from DC goes in different direction and only those photons that have absorbed by dye are appropriate. In our design, we solve these to problems. We use mirror followed by GQDs on the top of the Pt electrode. This has two advantages: first, it does not distribute inside light in to the cell and second, all of emitted light from DC back to the cell and can be absorbed by dye molecules (Schematic 1 ). Modified cell by GQDs shows 21% enhancement in JSC compare to the reference cell.

GC-2550TG (Teif Gostar Faraz Company, Iran) were used for all chemical analyses. In typical synthesis, 0.25g of corn powder was dispersed into 25mL DI water and stirred for 30min. Then the solution was transferred into a Teflon lined stainless autoclave and heated at 180 °C for 48h. The final product was soluble in water. The solution was centrifuged to separate the deposit, the brown solution was graphene quantum dots. In addition, effect of reaction time on the PL of prepared graphene quantum dots was investigated. Two sample prepared in 8 and 48h were selected to apply in DSSCs which labeled as GQD1 a d GQD2, respectively.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of working electrodes and cell assembling
ce:section.ce:para: It was done according to the previous works [18]. Briefly, FTO-coated glass (with surface resistance of 13Ω/sq. purchased from Sigma Aldrich) plates were cleaned by sonication in a detergent solution, ethanol, and acetone for 30min. For fabrication of transparent TiO2 layer, TiO2 paste was deposited on cleaned FTO by doctor-blading. Final thickness was 10μm. The fabricated electrodes were separately immersed into dye solution in ethanol (0.5mM) and kept at 48°C for 24h to complete the sensitizer uptake. The dye-adsorbed TiO2 electrodes were then rinsed with acetonitrile and dried. A Pt coated FTO glass electrode was used as a counter electrode. Surlyn was used as spacer. A Pt coated FTO glass electrode was prepared as a counter electrode. The Pt electrode was placed over the dye-adsorbed composite electrode. Sealing was accomplished by pressing the two electrodes together on a double hot-plate at a temperature of about 110°C. The I3−/I− electrolyte (Dyesol) was injected into the cell just before sealing. In the case of modified cell including GQDs as DC, GQDs solution dropped on pace of mirror and dried in oven. Then it was stuck on the top of cell (schismatic 1).For this, first, GQDs solution was coated by casting method followed by draying in oven. Then it was stuck on the top of cell by using glue. Solar cell performance was studied by using class computerized digital multimeter (Ivium-n-Stat Multichannel potentiostat) and a variable load. A 300W metal xenon lamp (Luzchem) served as a simulated sun light source, under standard test conditions (STC) to give 1000W/m2 equivalent to 1 sun at AM 1.5 at the surface of the test cell.

First, we prepare GQD by using corn powder as starting material. This is a green starting material. Fig.1 shows the typical X-ray diffraction pattern for as-synthesized graphene quantum dots by using corn powder. The XRD patterns of GQDs shows a broad peak at around 24° corresponding to the (002) peak which is matched with JCPDS Card No, 75-0444 while the Corn powder shows a broad peak that is result of its amorphous structure.

In the following, GQDs were applied in DSSCs to show their effect on PCE. For this, we deposited GQDs on the surface of a piece of mirror and stuck it on the top of Pt electrode (Schismatic 1). Reference cell had same stricture without GQDs. We also used mirror on top of Pt electrode for reference cell to remove scattering effect of mirror in modified cells. Corresponding current density versus voltage (J−V) curve and details of reference cells and those including GQDs are reported in Fig. 5 and Table 1 .

According to the Fig. 5 and Table 1, average PCE, Jsc, VOC, and FF for cells based on GQD1 and GQD2 were 6.90, 19.50, 0.654, 0.53 and 7.82, 22.62, 0.653, 0.53, respectively while for reference cells were 6.81, 18.60, 0.656, and 0.54. As seen from the results, PCE and JSC increased by 14.8% and 21.6% when GQDs are applied in DSSCs as DC.

Table 3 shows the results of IMVS and IMPS. As seen from references cells and modified cells have close recombination times, and charge-collection efficiency. This is another evidence that this enhancement is because of down conversion effects of graphene quantum dots.

We used AFM to evaluate topology of coated GQDs on the backside of cells. For this, we dropped GQD solution on the slide glass and dried it then we took AFM. Fig. 7 shows related AFM images. As seen, AFM results show average roughness of 67nm.

To conclude, graphene quantum dots were prepared by using a green starting material (corn powder).This prepared GQDs show significant luminance that convert UV light to the visible light. We used this properties to improve light harvesting in DSSCs. Solar cells based on GQDs as DC show much higher power conversion efficiency compare to the reference cells. We believe that the results of our study offer an effective approach to enhance light harvesting in different optical devices that cannot absorb high energy photon.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: Authors are grateful to the council of Iran National Science Foundation (INSF) and University of Kashan for supporting this work by Grant No (159271/899000).
